revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:شيكي كيتاكاتسوراغي]][[ca:Districte de Kita-Katsuragi]][[en:Kitakatsuragi District, Nara]][[es:Distrito de Kitakatsuragi]][[fr:District de Kitakatsuragi]][[id:Distrik Kitakatsuragi, Nara]][[it:Distretto di Kitakatsuragi]][[ja:北葛城郡]][[ko:기타카쓰라기군]][[nl:Kitakatsuragi (district)]][[pl:Powiat Kitakatsuragi]][[ru:Китакацураги (уезд)]][[zh:北葛城郡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1131013]] Wikidata name: Kitakatsuragi district [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q1131013]] Wikidata name: Kitakatsuragi district [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Kitakatsuragi District in Nara prefecture Ja.png|thumb|250px|Location of Kitakatsuragi District in Nara Prefecture]] {{nihongo|'''Kitakatsuragi'''|北葛城郡|Kitakatsuragi-gun}} is a [[:en:Districts of Japan|district]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
As of 2003, the district has an estimated [[:en:Population|Population]] of 134,816 and a [[:en:Population density|density]] of 1,886.07 persons per km<sup>2</sup>. The total area is 71.48 km<sup>2</sup>.
==Towns and villages== *[[:en:Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]] *[[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]] *[[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]] *[[:en:Ōji, Nara|Ōji]]
==Mergers== *On October 1, 2004 the towns of [[:en:Shinjō, Nara|Shinjō]] and [[:en:Taima, Nara|Taima]] merged to form the new city of [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=総務省｜令和2nd year 版 地方財政白書｜資料編 〔附属資料〕 昭和60th year 度以降の市町村合併の実績 |trans-title=Record of Municipal Mergers Since Showa 60 |url=https://www.soumu.go.jp/menu_seisaku/hakusyo/chihou/32data/2020data/r02czs05-03.html |access-date=2024-02-28 |website=総務省 |language=ja}}</ref>
== References == {{Refs}}{{Nara Prefecture}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ar:شيكي كيتاكاتسوراغي]][[ca:Districte de Kita-Katsuragi]][[en:Kitakatsuragi District, Nara]][[es:Distrito de Kitakatsuragi]][[fr:District de Kitakatsuragi]][[id:Distrik Kitakatsuragi, Nara]][[it:Distretto di Kitakatsuragi]][[ja:北葛城郡]][[ko:기타카쓰라기군]][[nl:Kitakatsuragi (district)]][[pl:Powiat Kitakatsuragi]][[ru:Китакацураги (уезд)]][[zh:北葛城郡]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1131013]] Wikidata name: Kitakatsuragi district [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q1131013]] Wikidata name: Kitakatsuragi district [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[Image:Kitakatsuragi District in Nara prefecture Ja.png|thumb|250px|Location of Kitakatsuragi District in Nara Prefecture]] {{nihongo|'''Kitakatsuragi'''|北葛城郡|Kitakatsuragi-gun}} is a [[:en:Districts of Japan|district]] located in [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]].
As of 2003, the district has an estimated [[:en:Population|Population]] of 134,816 and a [[:en:Population density|density]] of 1,886.07 persons per km<sup>2</sup>. The total area is 71.48 km<sup>2</sup>.
==Towns and villages== *[[:en:Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]] *[[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]] *[[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]] *[[:en:Ōji, Nara|Ōji]]
==Mergers== *On October 1, 2004 the towns of [[:en:Shinjō, Nara|Shinjō]] and [[:en:Taima, Nara|Taima]] merged to form the new city of [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=総務省｜令和2nd year 版 地方財政白書｜資料編 〔附属資料〕 昭和60th year 度以降の市町村合併の実績 |trans-title=Record of Municipal Mergers Since Showa 60 |url=https://www.soumu.go.jp/menu_seisaku/hakusyo/chihou/32data/2020data/r02czs05-03.html |access-date=2024-02-28 |website=総務省 |language=ja}}</ref>

As of 2003, the district has an estimated [[:en:Population|Population]] of 134,816 and a [[:en:Population density|density]] of 1,886.07 persons per km<sup>2</sup>. The total area is 71.48 km<sup>2</sup>.
==Towns and villages== *[[:en:Kanmaki, Nara|Kanmaki]] *[[:en:Kawai, Nara|Kawai]] *[[:en:Kōryō, Nara|Kōryō]] *[[:en:Ōji, Nara|Ōji]]
==Mergers== *On October 1, 2004 the towns of [[:en:Shinjō, Nara|Shinjō]] and [[:en:Taima, Nara|Taima]] merged to form the new city of [[:en:Katsuragi, Nara|Katsuragi]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=総務省｜令和2nd year 版 地方財政白書｜資料編 〔附属資料〕 昭和60th year 度以降の市町村合併の実績 |trans-title=Record of Municipal Mergers Since Showa 60 |url=https://www.soumu.go.jp/menu_seisaku/hakusyo/chihou/32data/2020data/r02czs05-03.html |access-date=2024-02-28 |website=総務省 |language=ja}}</ref>

As of 2003, the district has an estimated {{ill|Population|en|Population|WD=Q2625603|lt=Population}} of 134,816 and a {{ill|Population density|en|Population density|WD=Q22856|lt=density}} of 1,886.07 persons per km<sup>2</sup>. The total area is 71.48 km<sup>2</sup>.
==Towns and villages== *{{ill|Kanmaki, Nara|en|Kanmaki, Nara|WD=Q1203559|lt=Kanmaki}} *{{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|WD=Q1203880|lt=Kawai}} *{{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|WD=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō}} *{{ill|Ōji, Nara|en|Ōji, Nara|WD=Q1346392|lt=Ōji}}
==Mergers== *On October 1, 2004 the towns of {{ill|Shinjō, Nara|en|Shinjō, Nara|WD=Q1306429|lt=Shinjō}} and {{ill|Taima, Nara|en|Taima, Nara|WD=Q7676155|lt=Taima}} merged to form the new city of {{ill|Katsuragi, Nara|en|Katsuragi, Nara|WD=Q659672|lt=Katsuragi}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=総務省｜令和2nd year 版 地方財政白書｜資料編 〔附属資料〕 昭和60th year 度以降の市町村合併の実績 |trans-title=Record of Municipal Mergers Since Showa 60 |url=https://www.soumu.go.jp/menu_seisaku/hakusyo/chihou/32data/2020data/r02czs05-03.html |access-date=2024-02-28 |website=総務省 |language=ja}}</ref>

[[Image:Kitakatsuragi District in Nara prefecture Ja.png|thumb|250px|Location of Kitakatsuragi District in Nara Prefecture]] {{nihongo|'''Kitakatsuragi'''|北葛城郡|Kitakatsuragi-gun}} is a {{ill|Districts of Japan|en|Districts of Japan|qid=Q1122846|lt=district|1=Districts of Japan}} located in {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|qid=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.
As of 2003, the district has an estimated {{ill|Population|en|Population|qid=Q2625603|lt=Population|1=Population}} of 134,816 and a {{ill|Population density|en|Population density|qid=Q22856|lt=density|1=Population density}} of 1,886.07 persons per km<sup>2</sup>. The total area is 71.48 km<sup>2</sup>.
==Towns and villages== *{{ill|Kanmaki, Nara|en|Kanmaki, Nara|qid=Q1203559|lt=Kanmaki|1=Kanmaki, Nara}} *{{ill|Kawai, Nara|en|Kawai, Nara|qid=Q1203880|lt=Kawai|1=Kawai, Nara}} *{{ill|Kōryō, Nara|en|Kōryō, Nara|qid=Q1346830|lt=Kōryō|1=Kōryō, Nara}} *{{ill|Ōji, Nara|en|Ōji, Nara|qid=Q1346392|lt=Ōji|1=Ōji, Nara}}
==Mergers== *On October 1, 2004 the towns of {{ill|Shinjō, Nara|en|Shinjō, Nara|qid=Q1306429|lt=Shinjō|1=Shinjō, Nara}} and {{ill|Taima, Nara|en|Taima, Nara|qid=Q7676155|lt=Taima|1=Taima, Nara}} merged to form the new city of {{ill|Katsuragi, Nara|en|Katsuragi, Nara|qid=Q659672|lt=Katsuragi|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=総務省｜令和2nd year 版 地方財政白書｜資料編 〔附属資料〕 昭和60th year 度以降の市町村合併の実績 |trans-title=Record of Municipal Mergers Since Showa 60 |url=https://www.soumu.go.jp/menu_seisaku/hakusyo/chihou/32data/2020data/r02czs05-03.html |access-date=2024-02-28 |website=総務省 |language=ja}}</ref>
